title: Atherectomy Devices Market Size by Segments, Share, Regulatory, Reimbursement, Interventions and Forecast to 2036
author: Researchandmarkets.Com
description: A comprehensive market analysis report on atherectomy devices covering 39 countries with forecasts through 2036. The market is driven by the shift toward minimally-invasive techniques for treating coronary and peripheral artery diseases, replacing traditional bypass surgery. The report provides detailed market segmentation, intervention volumes, competitive dynamics, and regulatory insights across developed markets.

Key competitor in the expanding atherectomy devices market, positioned to benefit from increased adoption of minimally-invasive techniques for coronary and peripheral artery disease treatment.

title: Why AngioDynamics Stock Is Sinking Today
author: Keith Speights
description: AngioDynamics shares fell 13.1% despite posting strong Q3 results with revenue of $79.4 million (beating estimates) and better-than-expected earnings. The sell-off was triggered by the unexpected announcement of CEO Jim Clemmer's retirement after 10 years leading the company. While the board has formed a search committee to find a successor and the company raised its 2026 guidance, investor uncertainty about leadership transition overshadowed the positive business fundamentals.

insights.sentiment_reasoning: Stock declined 13.1% due to unexpected CEO retirement announcement creating leadership uncertainty, despite strong Q3 financial results (8.8% revenue growth, earnings beat) and positive business developments (FDA clearances and raised 2026 guidance). The market's negative reaction reflects investor concern about the leadership vacuum rather than fundamental business deterioration.

title: Why Did AngioDynamics Stock Pop Today?
author: Rich Smith
description: AngioDynamics reported Q1 fiscal 2026 results with mixed performance, beating adjusted earnings expectations but showing ongoing challenges in profitability. The company saw 12% overall sales growth, driven by med tech segment, but remains years away from consistent profitability.

insights.sentiment_reasoning: Stock rose 10.5% after beating adjusted earnings expectations, but continues to report negative adjusted profits and remains far from consistent profitability. Mixed performance with strong med tech segment growth offset by flat medical devices performance.

title: 3 Stocks Insiders Are Buying Heavily in October and Why
author: The Tokenist
description: Insiders have been making significant purchases in Eton Pharmaceuticals, AngioDynamics, and Tile Shop Holdings, indicating their confidence in these companies' prospects.

insights.sentiment_reasoning: The company's CEO has made a significant insider purchase, indicating confidence in the company's medical device business and its focus on higher-margin products.

Insiders have made purchases, but the company's performance has been mixed, with declining sales in the current market environment. However, the company's cash position and potential for a market turnaround could provide upside.

title: AngioDynamics Loses -15.86% in 4 Weeks, Here's Why a Trend Reversal May be Around the Corner
author: Zacks, Benzinga Contributor
description: AngioDynamics (ANGO) has seen a 15.9% decline in the past four weeks, but its oversold RSI and improved earnings estimates suggest a potential turnaround may be on the horizon.

insights.sentiment_reasoning: The article suggests that AngioDynamics' stock is currently oversold, with an RSI of 24.7, indicating that the heavy selling pressure may be exhausting itself. Additionally, the company has seen an increase in earnings estimates over the past 30 days, which typically translates to price appreciation in the near term. The article also notes that AngioDynamics has a Zacks Rank #2 (Buy), further supporting the potential for a turnaround.
